• Show All Code
  • Hide All Code

GEOM WHIM Descriptor MAE QC

Michael Tran

2026-04-30

This notebook demonstrates the structure and basic use of the final MultiAssayExperiment containing GEOM-derived WHIM descriptor assays and compound annotations.

WHIM, or Weighted Holistic Invariant Molecular, descriptors summarize 3D molecular shape from conformer coordinates. In the pipeline, RDKit computes WHIM descriptors for GEOM conformers with valid Boltzmann weights, and the assay matrices store three compound-level summaries: the highest-probability conformer (WHIM_hp), the arithmetic mean across valid conformers (WHIM_avg), and the Boltzmann-weighted mean across valid conformers (WHIM_wavg).

WHIM descriptors were introduced by Todeschini, Gramatica, Provenzani, and Marengo in Chemometrics and Intelligent Laboratory Systems: https://doi.org/10.1016/0169-7439(95)80026-6.

Setup

Load the MAE

mae <- readRDS(mae_path)

assay_names <- names(experiments(mae))
assay_mats <- setNames(
  lapply(assay_names, \(assay_name) assay(experiments(mae)[[assay_name]])),
  assay_names
)

compound_metadata <- as.data.frame(metadata(mae)$Drug.Metadata) |>
  rownames_to_column("Compound.Key") |>
  as_tibble()

coldata <- as.data.frame(colData(mae)) |>
  as_tibble()

rowdata <- bind_rows(
  lapply(assay_names, \(assay_name) {
    as.data.frame(rowData(experiments(mae)[[assay_name]])) |>
      as_tibble() |>
      mutate(Assay = assay_name)
  })
)

mae
## A MultiAssayExperiment object of 3 listed
##  experiments with user-defined names and respective classes.
##  Containing an ExperimentList class object of length 3:
##  [1] WHIM_hp: SummarizedExperiment with 114 rows and 304348 columns
##  [2] WHIMS_avg: SummarizedExperiment with 114 rows and 304348 columns
##  [3] WHIMS_wavg: SummarizedExperiment with 114 rows and 304348 columns
## Functionality:
##  experiments() - obtain the ExperimentList instance
##  colData() - the primary/phenotype DataFrame
##  sampleMap() - the sample coordination DataFrame
##  `$`, `[`, `[[` - extract colData columns, subset, or experiment
##  *Format() - convert into a long or wide DataFrame
##  assays() - convert ExperimentList to a SimpleList of matrices
##  exportClass() - save data to flat files

Dataset Overview

assay_summary <- tibble(
  assay = assay_names,
  assay_name = vapply(
    assay_names,
    \(assay_name) {
      paste(assayNames(experiments(mae)[[assay_name]]), collapse = ", ")
    },
    character(1)
  ),
  features = vapply(
    assay_names,
    \(assay_name) nrow(assay_mats[[assay_name]]),
    integer(1)
  ),
  compounds = vapply(
    assay_names,
    \(assay_name) ncol(assay_mats[[assay_name]]),
    integer(1)
  ),
  feature_type = "WHIM descriptor features"
)

annotation_availability <- compound_metadata |>
  mutate(
    annotation_status = if_else(
      In.AnnotationDB,
      "AnnotationDB enriched",
      "No AnnotationDB match",
      missing = "No AnnotationDB match"
    )
  ) |>
  count(annotation_status, name = "compounds") |>
  mutate(
    fraction = compounds / sum(compounds),
    percent = scales::percent(fraction, accuracy = 0.1),
    label = paste0(
      annotation_status,
      "\n",
      scales::comma(compounds),
      " (",
      percent,
      ")"
    )
  )

overview_tbl <- tibble(
  quantity = c(
    "Pipeline ID",
    "Pipeline version",
    "Experiments",
    "Assays",
    "Compounds in colData",
    "Drug metadata records",
    "Source subsets",
    "WHIM features per assay",
    "Total assay columns",
    "Compounds with PubChem CID",
    "Compounds without PubChem CID",
    "Median total conformers",
    "Median unique conformers"
  ),
  value = c(
    metadata(mae)$Pipeline$ID,
    metadata(mae)$Pipeline$Version,
    fmt_count(length(experiments(mae))),
    paste(assay_names, collapse = ", "),
    fmt_count(nrow(coldata)),
    fmt_count(nrow(compound_metadata)),
    paste(sort(unique(compound_metadata$GEOM.Source.Subset)), collapse = ", "),
    fmt_count(unique(assay_summary$features)),
    fmt_count(sum(assay_summary$compounds)),
    fmt_count(sum(!is.na(compound_metadata$Pubchem.CID))),
    fmt_count(sum(is.na(compound_metadata$Pubchem.CID))),
    fmt_count(stats::median(
      compound_metadata$GEOM.Total.Conformers,
      na.rm = TRUE
    )),
    fmt_count(stats::median(
      compound_metadata$GEOM.Unique.Conformers,
      na.rm = TRUE
    ))
  )
)

kable(overview_tbl)
quantity value
Pipeline ID geom
Pipeline version v2.0.0
Experiments 3
Assays WHIM_hp, WHIMS_avg, WHIMS_wavg
Compounds in colData 304,348
Drug metadata records 304,348
Source subsets drugs
WHIM features per assay 114
Total assay columns 913,044
Compounds with PubChem CID 213,015
Compounds without PubChem CID 91,333
Median total conformers 245
Median unique conformers 51 
kable(assay_summary)
assay assay_name features compounds feature_type
WHIM_hp values 114 304348 WHIM descriptor features
WHIMS_avg values 114 304348 WHIM descriptor features
WHIMS_wavg values 114 304348 WHIM descriptor features

This MAE contains three WHIM descriptor assays. Rows are descriptor features and columns are compounds keyed by GEOM.Source.SMILES. The object is compound-level: each assay column represents one GEOM drug compound.

Inspect the MAE Structure

The object layout is inspected directly below. The experiment container, assay names, compound-level metadata, sample map, and object-level metadata can all be accessed from the printed structure and the code shown in this section.

mae
## A MultiAssayExperiment object of 3 listed
##  experiments with user-defined names and respective classes.
##  Containing an ExperimentList class object of length 3:
##  [1] WHIM_hp: SummarizedExperiment with 114 rows and 304348 columns
##  [2] WHIMS_avg: SummarizedExperiment with 114 rows and 304348 columns
##  [3] WHIMS_wavg: SummarizedExperiment with 114 rows and 304348 columns
## Functionality:
##  experiments() - obtain the ExperimentList instance
##  colData() - the primary/phenotype DataFrame
##  sampleMap() - the sample coordination DataFrame
##  `$`, `[`, `[[` - extract colData columns, subset, or experiment
##  *Format() - convert into a long or wide DataFrame
##  assays() - convert ExperimentList to a SimpleList of matrices
##  exportClass() - save data to flat files
experiments(mae)
## ExperimentList class object of length 3:
##  [1] WHIM_hp: SummarizedExperiment with 114 rows and 304348 columns
##  [2] WHIMS_avg: SummarizedExperiment with 114 rows and 304348 columns
##  [3] WHIMS_wavg: SummarizedExperiment with 114 rows and 304348 columns
colnames(colData(mae))
##  [1] "GEOM.Source.SMILES"     "Pubchem.CID"            "InChIKey"              
##  [4] "GEOM.RDKit.SMILES"      "In.AnnotationDB"        "AnnotationDB.Name"     
##  [7] "AnnotationDB.SMILES"    "GEOM.Source.Subset"     "GEOM.Canonical.SMILES" 
## [10] "GEOM.Charge"            "GEOM.Total.Conformers"  "GEOM.Unique.Conformers"
## [13] "GEOM.Lowest.Energy"     "GEOM.Ensemble.Energy"
head(as.data.frame(sampleMap(mae)))
##     assay                                             primary
## 1 WHIM_hp BrC(/C=C\\c1ccccc1)=N/Nc1nc(N2CCOCC2)nc(N2CCOCC2)n1
## 2 WHIM_hp            BrC(/C=N/N1CCN(Cc2ccccc2)CC1)=C/c1ccccc1
## 3 WHIM_hp  BrC(/C=N/Nc1nc(N2CCOCC2)nc(N2CCOCC2)n1)=C/c1ccccc1
## 4 WHIM_hp           BrC(=C\\c1ccccc1)/C=N/N1CCN(Cc2ccccc2)CC1
## 5 WHIM_hp            BrC(=C\\c1ccccc1)/C=N/N1CCN(c2ccccc2)CC1
## 6 WHIM_hp            BrC(=C\\c1ccccc1)/C=N/N1CCN(c2ccccn2)CC1
##                                               colname
## 1 BrC(/C=C\\c1ccccc1)=N/Nc1nc(N2CCOCC2)nc(N2CCOCC2)n1
## 2            BrC(/C=N/N1CCN(Cc2ccccc2)CC1)=C/c1ccccc1
## 3  BrC(/C=N/Nc1nc(N2CCOCC2)nc(N2CCOCC2)n1)=C/c1ccccc1
## 4           BrC(=C\\c1ccccc1)/C=N/N1CCN(Cc2ccccc2)CC1
## 5            BrC(=C\\c1ccccc1)/C=N/N1CCN(c2ccccc2)CC1
## 6            BrC(=C\\c1ccccc1)/C=N/N1CCN(c2ccccn2)CC1
names(metadata(mae))
## [1] "Pipeline"      "Drug.Metadata"

The sampleMap has one row for each assay-compound pair. For every assay, both primary and colname are GEOM.Source.SMILES, so the same compound metadata row maps to that compound’s column in each WHIM assay.

Compound Metadata

compound_metadata |>
  select(
    GEOM.Source.SMILES,
    Pubchem.CID,
    InChIKey,
    In.AnnotationDB,
    AnnotationDB.Name,
    GEOM.Source.Subset,
    GEOM.Charge,
    GEOM.Total.Conformers,
    GEOM.Unique.Conformers,
    GEOM.Lowest.Energy,
    GEOM.Ensemble.Energy
  ) |>
  slice_head(n = 8) |>
  kable()
GEOM.Source.SMILES Pubchem.CID InChIKey In.AnnotationDB AnnotationDB.Name GEOM.Source.Subset GEOM.Charge GEOM.Total.Conformers GEOM.Unique.Conformers GEOM.Lowest.Energy GEOM.Ensemble.Energy
BrC(/C=Cccccc1)=N/Nc1nc(N2CCOCC2)nc(N2CCOCC2)n1 NA QEJRAQJWLWLEOF-PRMXUTLTSA-N FALSE NA drugs 0 83 27 -87.80248 0.282
BrC(/C=N/N1CCN(Cc2ccccc2)CC1)=C/c1ccccc1 6879954 DYNORDCFBLDDQF-RQRAPYSXSA-N TRUE (E)-4-benzyl-N-((E)-2-bromo-3-phenylallylidene)piperazin-1-amine drugs 0 351 130 -66.85172 0.479
BrC(/C=N/Nc1nc(N2CCOCC2)nc(N2CCOCC2)n1)=C/c1ccccc1 6877346 VOIWWGCVDFCTAW-RTMICUHXSA-N TRUE N-(2-Bromo-3-phenyl-allylidene)-N’-(4,6-di-morpholin-4-yl-[1,3,5]triazin-2-yl)-hydrazine drugs 0 34 10 -87.79648 0.169
BrC(=Cccccc1)/C=N/N1CCN(Cc2ccccc2)CC1 5342758 DYNORDCFBLDDQF-IJDGKNKPSA-N TRUE 4-benzyl-N-(2-bromo-3-phenyl-2-propen-1-ylidene)-1-piperazinamine drugs 0 319 141 -66.85031 0.296
BrC(=Cccccc1)/C=N/N1CCN(c2ccccc2)CC1 5727615 JASQRDIPLVCSQS-TXWVUNSISA-N TRUE N-(2-Bromo-3-phenyl-2-propenylidene)-4-phenyl-1-piperazinamine drugs 0 176 23 -63.69226 0.384
BrC(=Cccccc1)/C=N/N1CCN(c2ccccn2)CC1 6897665 FZNNISGQTKDUIM-WANMUPLOSA-N TRUE N-(2-bromo-3-phenyl-2-propen-1-ylidene)-4-(2-pyridinyl)-1-piperazinamine drugs 0 171 66 -63.97501 0.509
BrC(=Cccccc1)/C=N/Nc1ccc2nncn2n1 6862203 JTLAOTXPOJNRJW-OOBCCFMBSA-N TRUE N-[(E)-[(Z)-2-bromo-3-phenylprop-2-enylidene]amino]-[1,2,4]triazolo[4,3-b]pyridazin-6-amine drugs 0 24 7 -57.13764 0.206
BrC(=Cccccc1)/C=N/Nc1nc(N2CCOCC2)nc(N2CCOCC2)n1 6877347 VOIWWGCVDFCTAW-WKSPYJHRSA-N TRUE N-(2-Bromo-3-phenyl-allylidene)-N’-(4,6-di-morpholin-4-yl-[1,3,5]triazin-2-yl)-h drugs 0 162 28 -87.79328 0.277

The main fields commonly used in downstream analysis are:

  • GEOM.Source.SMILES: the GEOM source molecule key used as the MAE primary key and assay column name.
  • Pubchem.CID: the PubChem compound identifier used to join back to the base harmonized drug dataset.
  • InChIKey: the structure-level identifier derived from the representative GEOM conformer molecule.
  • GEOM.RDKit.SMILES and GEOM.Canonical.SMILES: RDKit-derived structures retained for comparison with the source key.
  • In.AnnotationDB: whether the compound mapped to AnnotationDB metadata.
  • GEOM.Total.Conformers and GEOM.Unique.Conformers: GEOM conformer counts carried into the compound-level metadata.
  • GEOM.Lowest.Energy and GEOM.Ensemble.Energy: GEOM energy summaries for the compound.
metadata_completeness <- compound_metadata |>
  summarise(
    across(
      c(
        Pubchem.CID,
        InChIKey,
        GEOM.RDKit.SMILES,
        GEOM.Canonical.SMILES,
        AnnotationDB.Name,
        AnnotationDB.SMILES,
        GEOM.Charge,
        GEOM.Total.Conformers,
        GEOM.Unique.Conformers,
        GEOM.Lowest.Energy,
        GEOM.Ensemble.Energy
      ),
      \(value) sum(is_present(value))
    )
  ) |>
  pivot_longer(
    cols = everything(),
    names_to = "field",
    values_to = "present"
  ) |>
  mutate(
    missing = nrow(compound_metadata) - present,
    percent_present = fmt_percent(present / nrow(compound_metadata))
  ) |>
  arrange(desc(present))

kable(metadata_completeness)
field present missing percent_present

GEOM.Charge 304348 0 100.0%
InChIKey 304346 2 100.0%
GEOM.Total.Conformers 304294 54 100.0%
GEOM.Unique.Conformers 304294 54 100.0%
GEOM.Lowest.Energy 304294 54 100.0%
GEOM.Ensemble.Energy 304294 54 100.0%
Pubchem.CID 213015 91333 70.0%
AnnotationDB.SMILES 213015 91333 70.0%
AnnotationDB.Name 211416 92932 69.5% 
kable(
  annotation_availability |>
    select(annotation_status, compounds, percent)
)
annotation_status compounds percent
AnnotationDB enriched 213015 70.0%
No AnnotationDB match 91333 30.0% 
ggplot(
  annotation_availability,
  aes(x = "", y = fraction, fill = annotation_status)
) +
  geom_col(width = 1, color = "white") +
  geom_text(
    aes(label = label),
    position = position_stack(vjust = 0.5),
    lineheight = 0.95
  ) +
  coord_polar(theta = "y") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("#4C956C", "#C75C5C")) +
  labs(
    title = "AnnotationDB metadata availability for GEOM compounds",
    fill = NULL
  ) +
  theme_void()

Conformer Landscape

GEOM provides conformer-level structure summaries. The MAE stores compound-level conformer counts and energy summaries in colData.

conformer_summary_tbl <- compound_metadata |>
  summarise(
    compounds = n(),
    total_conformer_min = min(GEOM.Total.Conformers, na.rm = TRUE),
    total_conformer_median = stats::median(GEOM.Total.Conformers, na.rm = TRUE),
    total_conformer_mean = mean(GEOM.Total.Conformers, na.rm = TRUE),
    total_conformer_max = max(GEOM.Total.Conformers, na.rm = TRUE),
    unique_conformer_min = min(GEOM.Unique.Conformers, na.rm = TRUE),
    unique_conformer_median = stats::median(
      GEOM.Unique.Conformers,
      na.rm = TRUE
    ),
    unique_conformer_mean = mean(GEOM.Unique.Conformers, na.rm = TRUE),
    unique_conformer_max = max(GEOM.Unique.Conformers, na.rm = TRUE)
  ) |>
  mutate(across(everything(), fmt_number))

kable(conformer_summary_tbl)
compounds total_conformer_min total_conformer_median total_conformer_mean total_conformer_max unique_conformer_min unique_conformer_median unique_conformer_mean unique_conformer_max
304,348 1 245 389.03 11,734 1 51 102.61 7,451 
compound_metadata |>
  transmute(
    `Total conformers` = pmax(GEOM.Total.Conformers, 1),
    `Unique conformers` = pmax(GEOM.Unique.Conformers, 1)
  ) |>
  pivot_longer(
    cols = everything(),
    names_to = "count_type",
    values_to = "conformers"
  ) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = conformers, fill = count_type)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 50, alpha = 0.7, position = "identity") +
  scale_x_log10(labels = scales::comma) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("#8EA8C3", "#D49A3A")) +
  labs(
    title = "GEOM conformer count distribution",
    x = "Conformers per compound",
    y = "Number of compounds",
    fill = NULL
  )

compound_metadata |>
  filter(is.finite(GEOM.Lowest.Energy)) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = GEOM.Lowest.Energy)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 70, fill = "#3B6EA8", color = "white") +
  labs(
    title = "GEOM lowest conformer energy",
    x = "Lowest energy",
    y = "Number of compounds"
  )

compound_metadata |>
  filter(is.finite(GEOM.Ensemble.Energy)) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = GEOM.Ensemble.Energy)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 70, fill = "#6E9F64", color = "white") +
  labs(
    title = "GEOM ensemble energy",
    x = "Ensemble energy",
    y = "Number of compounds"
  )

compound_metadata |>
  count(GEOM.Charge, name = "compounds") |>
  ggplot(aes(x = factor(GEOM.Charge), y = compounds)) +
  geom_col(fill = "#7D5BA6") +
  scale_y_log10(labels = scales::comma) +
  labs(
    title = "Compound charge distribution",
    x = "GEOM charge",
    y = "Number of compounds"
  )

compound_metadata |>
  arrange(desc(GEOM.Total.Conformers), GEOM.Source.SMILES) |>
  select(
    GEOM.Source.SMILES,
    Pubchem.CID,
    In.AnnotationDB,
    GEOM.Total.Conformers,
    GEOM.Unique.Conformers,
    GEOM.Lowest.Energy,
    GEOM.Ensemble.Energy
  ) |>
  slice_head(n = 12) |>
  kable()
GEOM.Source.SMILES Pubchem.CID In.AnnotationDB GEOM.Total.Conformers GEOM.Unique.Conformers GEOM.Lowest.Energy GEOM.Ensemble.Energy

WHIM Descriptor Assays

rowdata |>
  select(Assay, Feature.ID, Feature.Index, Assay.Name) |>
  filter(Assay == "WHIM_hp") |>
  slice_head(n = 6) |>
  kable()
Assay Feature.ID Feature.Index Assay.Name
WHIM_hp WHIM_001 1 WHIM_hp
WHIM_hp WHIM_002 2 WHIM_hp
WHIM_hp WHIM_003 3 WHIM_hp
WHIM_hp WHIM_004 4 WHIM_hp
WHIM_hp WHIM_005 5 WHIM_hp
WHIM_hp WHIM_006 6 WHIM_hp 
assay_quality_tbl <- bind_rows(
  lapply(assay_names, \(assay_name) {
    mat <- assay_mats[[assay_name]]
    sampled_values <- sample_values(mat)
    tibble(
      assay = assay_name,
      features = nrow(mat),
      compounds = ncol(mat),
      entries = length(mat),
      missing_entries = sum(is.na(mat)),
      non_finite_entries = sum(!is.finite(mat)),
      sampled_min = min(sampled_values, na.rm = TRUE),
      sampled_median = stats::median(sampled_values, na.rm = TRUE),
      sampled_mean = mean(sampled_values, na.rm = TRUE),
      sampled_max = max(sampled_values, na.rm = TRUE)
    )
  })
) |>
  mutate(
    across(
      c(features, compounds, entries, missing_entries, non_finite_entries),
      fmt_count
    ),
    across(
      c(sampled_min, sampled_median, sampled_mean, sampled_max),
      \(value) fmt_number(value, 4)
    )
  )

kable(assay_quality_tbl)
assay features compounds entries missing_entries non_finite_entries sampled_min sampled_median sampled_mean sampled_max
WHIM_hp 114 304,348 34,695,672 0 0 0 0.4960 12.7996 2,994.671
WHIMS_avg 114 304,348 34,695,672 0 0 0 0.5006 14.2520 3,030.899
WHIMS_wavg 114 304,348 34,695,672 0 0 0 0.4955 13.0628 2,997.577 
feature_stats |>
  group_by(Assay) |>
  slice_max(SD, n = 12, with_ties = FALSE) |>
  ungroup() |>
  mutate(
    Feature.Label = reorder(Feature.ID, SD)
  ) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = SD, y = Feature.Label, fill = Assay)) +
  geom_col(show.legend = FALSE) +
  facet_wrap(~Assay, scales = "free_y") +
  labs(
    title = "Most variable WHIM descriptor features",
    x = "Standard deviation across compounds",
    y = "Feature"
  )

Descriptor-Space UMAP

The UMAP below is a visualization of a reproducible sample of compounds using all three WHIM assay families. Each point is one compound-level descriptor profile.

set.seed(20260430)

umap_compound_source <- compound_metadata |>
  filter(GEOM.Source.SMILES %in% colnames(assay_mats[[assay_names[[1L]]]]))

umap_sample_n <- min(3000L, nrow(umap_compound_source))

umap_compounds <- umap_compound_source |>
  slice_sample(n = umap_sample_n)

umap_input <- do.call(
  cbind,
  lapply(assay_names, \(assay_name) {
    mat <- assay_mats[[assay_name]][,
      umap_compounds$GEOM.Source.SMILES,
      drop = FALSE
    ]
    t(mat)
  })
)

umap_input <- scale(umap_input)
umap_input[is.na(umap_input)] <- 0

umap_layout <- uwot::umap(
  umap_input,
  n_neighbors = 15,
  min_dist = 0.2,
  metric = "cosine",
  n_threads = 1,
  n_sgd_threads = 1,
  verbose = FALSE
)

umap_tbl <- as_tibble(umap_layout, .name_repair = \(x) c("UMAP1", "UMAP2")) |>
  bind_cols(
    umap_compounds |>
      select(
        GEOM.Source.SMILES,
        GEOM.Unique.Conformers,
        GEOM.Total.Conformers
      )
  ) |>
  mutate(Unique.Conformers.For.Color = pmax(GEOM.Unique.Conformers, 1))

ggplot(
  umap_tbl,
  aes(
    x = UMAP1,
    y = UMAP2,
    color = Unique.Conformers.For.Color
  )
) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.75, size = 1.2) +
  scale_color_gradient(
    low = "#D8E7F1",
    high = "#1F5F8B",
    trans = "log10",
    labels = scales::comma
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "UMAP of sampled GEOM WHIM descriptor profiles",
    subtitle = paste0(
      "Compound-level descriptors for ",
      scales::comma(nrow(umap_tbl)),
      " sampled compounds."
    ),
    x = "UMAP 1",
    y = "UMAP 2",
    color = "Unique\nconformers"
  )

Assay Relationship Checks

The three WHIM assays use the same compounds and feature count, but summarize conformer information differently. The plot below compares normalized per-compound descriptor-vector distances between assay pairs.

assay_pairs <- tribble(
  ~pair                     , ~assay_a    , ~assay_b     , ~denominator_assay ,
  "WHIM_hp vs WHIMS_avg"    , "WHIM_hp"   , "WHIMS_avg"  , "WHIMS_avg"        ,
  "WHIM_hp vs WHIMS_wavg"   , "WHIM_hp"   , "WHIMS_wavg" , "WHIMS_wavg"       ,
  "WHIMS_avg vs WHIMS_wavg" , "WHIMS_avg" , "WHIMS_wavg" , "WHIMS_avg"
)

assay_distance_tbl <- bind_rows(
  lapply(seq_len(nrow(assay_pairs)), \(index) {
    difference <- assay_mats[[assay_pairs$assay_a[[index]]]] -
      assay_mats[[assay_pairs$assay_b[[index]]]]
    absolute_distance <- sqrt(colSums(difference * difference, na.rm = TRUE))
    denominator_norm <- sqrt(
      colSums(
        assay_mats[[assay_pairs$denominator_assay[[index]]]]^2,
        na.rm = TRUE
      )
    )

    tibble(
      pair = assay_pairs$pair[[index]],
      normalized_distance = absolute_distance /
        pmax(
          denominator_norm,
          .Machine$double.eps
        )
    )
  })
)

assay_distance_tbl |>
  filter(normalized_distance > 0) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = normalized_distance, fill = pair)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 80, color = "white", show.legend = FALSE) +
  facet_wrap(~pair, scales = "free_y") +
  scale_y_log10(labels = scales::comma) +
  labs(
    title = "Normalized pairwise distances between WHIM assay summaries",
    subtitle = "Exact-zero distances are omitted to show the non-zero spread.",
    x = "Normalized descriptor-vector distance",
    y = "Number of compounds"
  )

High-Variance Descriptor Snapshot

This heatmap shows the most variable descriptor rows across compounds with the largest number of unique conformers. Values are scaled by descriptor row for visualization.

top_heatmap_features <- feature_stats |>
  arrange(desc(SD)) |>
  slice_head(n = 36) |>
  mutate(Feature.Key = paste(Assay, Feature.ID, sep = ": "))

top_heatmap_compounds <- compound_metadata |>
  arrange(desc(GEOM.Unique.Conformers), GEOM.Source.SMILES) |>
  slice_head(n = 80) |>
  pull(GEOM.Source.SMILES)

heatmap_mat <- do.call(
  rbind,
  lapply(seq_len(nrow(top_heatmap_features)), \(index) {
    assay_name <- top_heatmap_features$Assay[[index]]
    feature_id <- top_heatmap_features$Feature.ID[[index]]
    values <- assay_mats[[assay_name]][
      feature_id,
      top_heatmap_compounds,
      drop = FALSE
    ]
    rownames(values) <- top_heatmap_features$Feature.Key[[index]]
    values
  })
)

heatmap_scaled <- t(scale(t(heatmap_mat)))
heatmap_scaled[!is.finite(heatmap_scaled)] <- 0

heatmap_tbl <- as.data.frame(heatmap_scaled) |>
  rownames_to_column("Feature.Key") |>
  as_tibble() |>
  pivot_longer(
    cols = -Feature.Key,
    names_to = "GEOM.Source.SMILES",
    values_to = "Scaled.Value"
  ) |>
  mutate(
    Feature.Key = factor(
      Feature.Key,
      levels = rev(top_heatmap_features$Feature.Key)
    ),
    GEOM.Source.SMILES = factor(
      GEOM.Source.SMILES,
      levels = top_heatmap_compounds
    )
  )

ggplot(
  heatmap_tbl,
  aes(x = GEOM.Source.SMILES, y = Feature.Key, fill = Scaled.Value)
) +
  geom_tile() +
  scale_fill_gradient2(
    low = "#3B6EA8",
    mid = "white",
    high = "#C75C5C",
    midpoint = 0
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Scaled high-variance WHIM descriptor features",
    x = "Compounds ordered by unique conformer count",
    y = "Assay and feature",
    fill = "Scaled\nvalue"
  ) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),
    panel.grid = element_blank()
  )

Basic Access Patterns

The MAE can be used directly as an analysis-ready compound descriptor resource. Most analyses will either use one WHIM assay at a time or concatenate assay families after confirming that the descriptor families are appropriate for the question.

example_compound <- compound_metadata |>
  filter(In.AnnotationDB) |>
  arrange(desc(GEOM.Unique.Conformers), GEOM.Source.SMILES) |>
  slice(1) |>
  pull(GEOM.Source.SMILES)

example_metadata <- compound_metadata |>
  filter(GEOM.Source.SMILES == example_compound) |>
  select(
    GEOM.Source.SMILES,
    Pubchem.CID,
    InChIKey,
    AnnotationDB.Name,
    GEOM.Total.Conformers,
    GEOM.Unique.Conformers,
    GEOM.Lowest.Energy,
    GEOM.Ensemble.Energy
  )

kable(example_metadata)
GEOM.Source.SMILES Pubchem.CID InChIKey AnnotationDB.Name GEOM.Total.Conformers GEOM.Unique.Conformers GEOM.Lowest.Energy GEOM.Ensemble.Energy
CCCCCCCCCCCCCCCC(=O)OC(C)C 8907 XUGNVMKQXJXZCD-UHFFFAOYSA-N Isopropyl Palmitate 11180 7451 -68.26109 1.276 
example_descriptors <- bind_rows(
  lapply(assay_names, \(assay_name) {
    tibble(
      Assay = assay_name,
      Feature.ID = rownames(assay_mats[[assay_name]]),
      value = assay_mats[[assay_name]][, example_compound]
    )
  })
) |>
  arrange(Assay, desc(abs(value))) |>
  group_by(Assay) |>
  slice_head(n = 3) |>
  ungroup()

kable(example_descriptors)
Assay Feature.ID value
WHIMS_avg WHIM_113 247.8403
WHIMS_avg WHIM_108 240.7502
WHIMS_avg WHIM_111 237.2047
WHIMS_wavg WHIM_113 184.0387
WHIMS_wavg WHIM_108 179.0185
WHIMS_wavg WHIM_111 175.2372
WHIM_hp WHIM_113 161.1130
WHIM_hp WHIM_108 156.5930
WHIM_hp WHIM_111 153.0700 
pubchem_subset <- compound_metadata |>
  filter(!is.na(Pubchem.CID)) |>
  slice_head(n = 100) |>
  pull(GEOM.Source.SMILES)

whim_hp_subset <- assay_mats[["WHIM_hp"]][, pubchem_subset, drop = FALSE]

tibble(
  subset = "First 100 PubChem-mapped GEOM compounds",
  compounds = ncol(whim_hp_subset),
  features = nrow(whim_hp_subset)
) |>
  kable()
subset compounds features
First 100 PubChem-mapped GEOM compounds 100 114 
tibble(
  object = c(
    "WHIM_hp feature_by_compound_matrix",
    "WHIM_hp compound_by_feature_matrix"
  ),
  rows = c(nrow(assay_mats[["WHIM_hp"]]), ncol(assay_mats[["WHIM_hp"]])),
  columns = c(ncol(assay_mats[["WHIM_hp"]]), nrow(assay_mats[["WHIM_hp"]]))
) |>
  mutate(
    rows = fmt_count(rows),
    columns = fmt_count(columns)
  ) |>
  kable()
object rows columns
WHIM_hp feature_by_compound_matrix 114 304,348
WHIM_hp compound_by_feature_matrix 304,348 114

The compound-by-feature orientation is typically the convenient format for machine learning or clustering workflows, while the feature-by-compound orientation matches the stored SummarizedExperiment assays.
